response.justification: The candidate has a broad engineering background with genuine depth in TypeScript/Node.js, systems programming, and developer tooling, but the role's core requirements are largely absent from the resume. Java appears only as a peripheral language in older roles, Kotlin is never mentioned, and there is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience. The National Instruments role involved an IDE (LabWindows/CVI) but it was C-based and does not translate to the JVM IDE domain required here. The fundamental skill mismatch makes this candidate unsuitable despite their overall engineering competence.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to operate on high-scale, complex systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby; multi-country pricing platform at Optilyz)
Prior exposure to IDE tooling concepts via NI LabWindows/CVI work and ongoing MCP/developer tooling contributions at re:cinq
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD advocacy, knowledge sharing through workshops and talks, open source maintainer
concerns: Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js; Java surfaces only as a secondary language in two older roles with no depth demonstrated
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience anywhere in the resume
No JVM internals knowledge, no Maven/Gradle build tooling experience, and no application server integration work present

key_factors.factor: Prior IDE and developer tooling work
